Import TensorFlow and keras_cv modules required for the task
Define constants for batch size, image dimensions, and number of classes
Load a sample dataset (if available) or create a synthetic dataset with random images that have bounding boxes in 'xyxy' format
Build the YOLOV8 detector model using `keras_cv.models.YOLOV8Detector(num_classes=num_classes, bounding_box_format="xyxy", backbone=backbone, fpn_depth=1)`
Compile the YOLOV8 model with Adam optimizer and `binary_crossentropy` for classification loss and `ciou` for box loss
Create a synthetic dataset generator that yields batches of images with corresponding bounding boxes
Prepare training and validation datasets (if using real data) or use the synthetic dataset generator
Initialize the COCO metric callback with the validation dataset and define save path for the best model
Train the YOLOV8 model on the synthetic dataset for a specified number of epochs, monitoring mAP score in the callback logs
Visualize detections using `visualize_detections(yolo, dataset=val_ds, bounding_box_format="xyxy")` to inspect if predictions are as expected
Assert that the model training results in an unexpected behavior or error (e.g., high loss values, infinite loop, etc.) during execution
